what_it_does: Defines a static HTML design-system preview card showing the V logo in three side-by-side placements: yellow on canvas, white on gold, and yellow on dark.
observations: The dsCard metadata declares group Brand, name V mark — three placements, a descriptive subtitle, and viewport 700x240.
The card is a three-column CSS grid with no gap, zero padding, and a 240px minimum height.
Each pane centers its contents with flexbox and uses 24px padding and a 10px vertical gap.
The three pane backgrounds use design tokens for canvas, accent gold, and dark backgrounds.
The logo images are fixed at 110px by 110px.
Small uppercase tags identify each color/background pairing.
The file references _card.css and two PNG logo assets.
standouts: It demonstrates the same mark across three contrasting brand contexts.
The preview relies on CSS custom properties for color and typography tokens.
The inline metadata frames the file as a design-system card.
conventions: Uses file-local inline CSS for the preview-specific layout.
Uses semantic class names for pane variants: canvas, gold, and dark.
Uses tokenized colors and font variables rather than hard-coded core theme values where available.
Keeps the body markup minimal and directly tied to the preview card.
concerns: The image alt text is only "V", which gives limited context.
The repeated pane markup is simple but fully duplicated.
Fixed image dimensions and three equal columns may be narrow on smaller rendered widths.
notes: A compact brand-mark specimen showing approved-looking logo/background pairings in a design-system preview frame.
questions: Are these the only intended V mark placements, or only examples for this card?
Should the alt text identify the mark more explicitly?
Is the viewport metadata used by a card renderer outside this file?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the file: it presents the V mark in three placements corresponding to the metadata subtitle.
intention_signals: It seems intended to document how the V mark should appear against canvas, gold, and dark brand surfaces using existing logo assets and design tokens.
novelty: The dsCard metadata embedded in an HTML comment is distinct within the file because it turns a standalone preview into a catalogable design-system specimen.
contribution: Contributes a file-local reference for V mark placement behavior across three background contexts, including asset choices, tokenized surfaces, label treatment, and preview sizing.
summary_for_embedding: Static HTML design-system card previewing a V brand mark in yellow-on-canvas, white-on-gold, and yellow-on-dark placements with tokenized styling.
intention_for_embedding: Built to show and catalog acceptable V mark/background pairings as a compact brand preview specimen.
